name: Change SpacetimeDB Reducer Domain Slice
title: Implement or Change a SpacetimeDB Reducer/Domain Slice
summary: Implementation-grade guidance for adding or changing a SpacetimeDB reducer backed by explicit domain, repository, mapper, and table boundaries.
description: Use this task when a SpacetimeDB Rust module needs a new reducer, table-backed mutation, or domain slice change.

outputs.title: Domain behavior
outputs.description: Aggregate, service, policy, or explicit note that the slice is platform-only.

outputs.title: SpacetimeDB slice
outputs.description: Rows, repositories, mappers, reducers, views, or reconciliation changes.

outputs.title: Verification
outputs.description: Tests, builds, or review notes for behavior and boundaries.

validations.title: Confirm slice boundary
criteria: The slice is classified as rich domain, thin domain service, or platform-only before editing.
Existing domain, spacetime, table, reducer, view, and repository conventions are identified.

validations.title: Review SpacetimeDB domain slice
passCriteria: Reducers contain platform orchestration rather than hidden business decisions.
Domain logic remains pure Rust when the slice has domain rules.
Table access and storage identity stay behind repositories and mappers.
Relevant tests or explicit infeasibility notes are present.

blocks.markdown: Implement `{{ task.input.reducerName }}` in slice `{{ task.input.sliceName }}` for behavior: {{ task.input.behavior }}.

items: Inspect the existing module wiring, domain mirror, SpacetimeDB slice, repository, mapper, reducer, authz, and view conventions before editing.
Classify the slice as rich domain, thin domain service, or platform-only; do not create a domain aggregate when there are no invariants to protect.
Put new domain rules in pure domain code and keep `spacetimedb` imports out of that code.
Add or update rows, indexes, mappers, repositories, reducers, and views in the SpacetimeDB slice as needed.
Use repository functions for table access and indexed lookups; avoid ad hoc full-table scans in hot reducer paths.
Add or update focused tests for domain rules, mapper round trips, reconciliation plans, and lockstep conversions where the change touches them.
Run cargo fmt --check and `{{ task.input.testCommand }}`; run cargo check or spacetime build if feasible, and record any infeasible checks.

criteria.text: Slice boundary classification is explicit and the implementation follows it.
Reducer `{{ task.input.reducerName }}` authorizes, loads, calls domain/platform operation, saves, and records side effects explicitly.
`{{ task.input.testCommand }}` and formatter checks were run when feasible.
